page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the system features 8 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512 (including VNNI and BF16), AES, and SHA_NI. Local storage is provided via a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU is present.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range. Memory performance is a notable strength; the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10%, and large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong performance in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also demonstrate strong performance. However, multi-threaded decompression and database operations align with average tiers. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference with a small 135M model achieves average speeds, whereas prompt processing for a medium 7B model is weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

The primary architectural tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which introduces potential resource contention compared to dedicated instances. However, this configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency by providing a dense 64.0 GB memory footprint and fast NVMe storage at a lower resource tier. This makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. Recommended workloads include memory-cached databases, medium-scale web serving, data buffering, and general-purpose applications requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios.
description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and top-tier memory performance, backed by strong large-block memory bandwidth. While its shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-core compute workloads, it offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy applications. The server is well-suited for memory-cached databases, web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring a high memory-to-core ratio.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa server with 8 shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering top-tier single-core and memory performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd features 8 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier memory performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and fast NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
64.0 GB RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation offering qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy tasks
Best suited for memory-cached databases, web serving, and data buffering
